feed.title: Current Watches, Warnings and Advisories for New Zealand issued by MetService
feed.rights: Copyright Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited, 2026. Licensed under Creative Commons BY 4.0.

entry.title: Heavy Rain Watch
entry.summary: Periods of heavy rain, with thunderstorms possible and a low risk of localised downpours. Rainfall amounts may approach warning criteria with a front early morning and then a trough late afternoon or early evening. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Watch
entry.summary: Periods of heavy rain, with thunderstorms possible. Rainfall amounts may approach warning criteria. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Expect 80 to 110 mm of rain. Peak rates of 15 to 25 mm/h in possible thunderstorms, and a low risk of 25 to 40 mm/hr in localised downpours, until early Sunday afternoon. Impact: Streams and rivers may rise rapidly. Surface flooding, slips, and difficult driving conditions possible. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Low.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Expect a further 30 to 50 mm of rain but 70 to 90 mm about the King Country, central plateau and Taranaki Maunga on top of what has already fallen. Peaks rates of 15 to 25 mm/h in possible thunderstorms, and a low risk of localised downpours of 25 to 40 mm/h this evening and overnight. Impact: Streams and rivers may rise rapidly. Surface flooding, slips, and difficult driving conditions possible. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Low.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Watch
entry.summary: A period of heavy rain, with possible thunderstorms and a low risk of localised downpours, amounts may approach warning criteria in localised areas. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate. Changes: Watch has been extended to cover the remainder of Taupo and Bay of Plenty (not already covered by a warning), also Waikato.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Watch
entry.summary: Periods of heavy rain with possible thunderstorms, and a low risk of localised downpours. Rainfall amounts may approach warning criteria in localised areas. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Expect a further 40 to 60 mm about the ranges but 10 to 30 mm elsewhere especially north of Wellington Harbour. Peak rates of 10 to 15 mm/hr about the ranges, easing this evening. Impact: Streams and rivers may rise rapidly. Surface flooding, slips, and difficult driving conditions possible. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Minimal. Changes: The chance of upgrade for this Orange Warning has been decreased to Minimal.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Period of snow showers expected about the top of the road Sunday morning. 1 to 2cm may accumulate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Severe Thunderstorm Warning
entry.summary:
At 08:37 pm, MetService weather radar detected severe thunderstorms near BENNEYDALE, WAIPA VALLEY and MANGAPEHI.
These severe thunderstorms are moving towards the southsoutheast, and are expected to lie near THE HAUHUNGAROA RANGE at 09:07 pm and near TURANGI, THE HAUHUNGAROA RANGE, LAKE TAUPO and KURATAU JUNCTION at 09:37 pm.
These thunderstorms are expected to be accompanied by very heavy rain.
Impact:
Very heavy rain can cause surface and/or flash flooding about streams, gullies and urban areas, and make driving conditions extremely hazardous.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited
